title: Credential Reuse Across Multiple Newly-Created Accounts
narrative: Fraudsters leverage stolen card credentials to fund multiple newly-created accounts across different platforms or services. The stolen card number, CVV, and billing address pass basic authorization checks because the credentials themselves are valid. However, the card is being used to establish accounts at a rate inconsistent with legitimate cardholder behavior. Each individual transaction appears normal in amount and timing, but card-level aggregation features reveal the credential is being distributed across far more unique email addresses and account identities than a typical cardholder would create. Device fingerprints show first-time pairings with the card, and the card's usage pattern across distinct email domains and new account registrations significantly exceeds normal consumer behavior. This pattern exploits the gap between valid credentials and legitimate account ownership.
indicators: C8 > 29 (p90), elevated count of unique email domains associated with the card
C13 > 24 (p90), card used across significantly more email addresses than typical
C14 > 7 (p90), card linked to many distinct billing addresses in rolling window
card1_distinct_addr1 > 5, card has been used with multiple unique billing addresses
TransactionAmt between 50 and 200, amount appears normal and non-suspicious
indicator_explanations: The card has been used with an unusually high number of different email domain types
The card appears on far more email addresses than a legitimate cardholder would use
The card has been paired with many different billing addresses recently
The device has never been seen with this card before
The card has appeared with multiple distinct billing addresses across recent transactions
The transaction amount falls within typical consumer spending ranges
summary: A valid card credential is being reused to create multiple new accounts across different email addresses and billing contexts, with each transaction appearing normal but card-level aggregations revealing abnormal distribution across identities.
reasoning: Legitimate cardholders rarely create multiple new accounts using the same card across diverse email domains and billing addresses in short timeframes. The combination of valid credentials with elevated email-domain diversity and new device pairings indicates credential theft and systematic account establishment.
